notes: Second production Level 2 run, picked as Candidate A from 12 TS/JS-pure non-Devin candidates. Direct comparison to cal.com (commit cedf388): different agent (100% OpenAI Codex vs 100% Devin), different shape (small TS library vs Next.js monorepo), different size (68 PRs vs 220). Skip rate dropped sharply from 81% (cal.com) to 21% (remix-forms) — confirms the cal.com skip rate was Devin-specific (force-pushed branches), not a general scanner limitation. Codex preserves parent commit SHAs much more reliably. Of the 54 scanned PRs: 11 with findings (20.4% finding rate vs cal.com's 64.3% — substantially lower), 9 F9 findings, 2 high-confidence security findings (hardcoded passwords in .spec.ts test files — first high-confidence findings from any Level 2 scan, classification target for triage: real credential leak vs legitimate test fixture). F9 pattern breakdown across the 9 findings: 5 'file not found' on .spec.ts files (suspect SI-003 multi-commit FP class), 3 'search string not found' on .test.ts/.tsx files (likely X-90 fabrications), 1 'ambiguous match' on .tsx file (X-91 territory). Per-PR JSONL stays Lenovo-local per data-separate-knowledge-unified policy. Triage pending operator review of raw data.
